perturbation.original_text: This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date"). Notwithstanding the aforementioned effective date, certain provisions, as explicitly delineated within, shall become effective 30 days following the aforementioned date and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the Third Amendment is effective as of July 3, 2019, but immediately introduces a caveat that *certain provisions* become effective 30 days later. This contradicts the initial statement of immediate effectiveness. The contradiction causes uncertainty in which provisions become effective immediately and which take 30 days to become effective. It affects the enforcement of any provision without a specific effective date, as there's no clear baseline, contradicting the whole point of having an effective date. This creates uncertainty in enforcement of the obligations under the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 2. Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the Third Amendment is effective as of July 3, 2019, but then adds that *certain provisions* become effective 30 days later. This conflicts with the contradicted text that states, 'all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.' The modification creates uncertainty about which specific provisions are immediately effective and which are delayed, directly contradicting the intent of maintaining all terms in full force from the effective date of the amendment.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Schedule C-1 in its entirety and replace it with the following: Schedule C-1. The following is attached to an incorporated into the Agreement as Schedule C-1: Schedule C-1([***]) [***]
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Schedule C-1 in its entirety and replace it with the following: Schedule C-1. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Schedule C-1 shall be retained and enforced until superseded by a future amendment. The following is attached to an incorporated into the Agreement as Schedule C-1: Schedule C-1([***]) [***]
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that Schedule C-1 is removed in its entirety while simultaneously stating that it is retained and enforced until a future amendment supersedes it. This creates significant confusion and ambiguity as to whether Schedule C-1 is actually in effect or not. The contradiction will affect any clause within the original agreement which refers to Schedule C-1, creating uncertainty about the legal basis for determining prices and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 2. Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states Schedule C-1 is removed and replaced. The changed text then states Schedule C-1 is both removed and retained until superseded. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text stating that all other terms of the agreement remain in full force and effect, as Schedule C-1's status (removed or retained) is now ambiguous, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as described by the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction".
